technology_orientation: skeptical of new workplace technology and worried it displaces useful human skills
pragmatic about workplace technology, valuing it when it clearly helps
enthusiastic about new workplace technology and quick to see productivity gains
ai_exposure: little direct exposure to AI tools
occasional exposure to AI tools through news, coworkers, or simple work tasks
frequent hands-on exposure to AI tools in work or learning
work_identity: identifies most with manual, trade, repair, or physical work
identifies most with service, care, retail, or hospitality work
identifies most with office, professional, education, or management work
is not currently employed or is retired, and evaluates work from outside the current labor force
skill_worldview: believes basic reliability and communication matter more than specialized technical skills
believes both practical interpersonal skills and technical skills matter
believes advanced analytical and technical skills are increasingly decisive
labor_market_security: feels economically secure and confident about adapting to labor-market change
feels some uncertainty about future work opportunities
feels economically vulnerable and worried about being left behind
